digest.component_or_area: audit web server scaffolding (rebar audit serve)
propositions: Add a default-false `[ui] enabled` config flag with full UiConfig wiring so it round-trips via rebar.toml, REBAR_UI_ENABLED, and -c override without being reported as an unknown key.
Provide a `rebar audit serve` subcommand registered as a CLI intercept that starts a FastAPI server serving a read-only index page listing tickets with audit data.
All web imports must be lazy so that with the feature disabled or the ui extra absent, `import rebar` pulls in no web dependency and stays inert.
When the ui extra is absent, `rebar audit serve` must exit non-zero with an actionable 'install nava-rebar[ui]' message rather than a traceback.
The server binds 127.0.0.1 loopback by default; binding a non-loopback host via `--host` prints a network-exposure warning to stderr.
The disabled default path must refuse to start the server with a message naming the flag.
